quotes.quote: To ensure the proposals are both grounded and new, this process is tightly integrated with a literature review module that queries external academic databases to identify knowledge gaps and avoid rediscovering existing work. The system uses structured prompts to guide idea generation and reflection rounds to refine proposals based on the literature search results (see Supplementary Information section A.2.6 for prompts).
quotes.rationale: Identifies an internal literature-review evaluator using academic databases to judge grounding and novelty and route results into proposal refinement.

quotes.quote: At the end of each stage, an LLM-based evaluator assesses all leaf nodes and selects the most promising one to serve as the root for the next stage of exploration, thus effectively pruning less promising research avenues.
quotes.rationale: Describes a stage-bound LLM evaluator that ranks leaf nodes and controls transition to the next exploration stage.

quotes.quote: During experimentation, the plots generated are fed to a VLM, which is prompted to act as a scientist and critique them. For example, it might flag nonsensical axes or issues in the quality of generated examples or suggest clearer ways to present the data. This feedback is used to generate new experimental nodes in the tree search aimed at addressing the identified issues.
quotes.rationale: Reports a VLM critique channel over generated plots whose findings create new experimental nodes.

quotes.quote: When choosing between non-buggy nodes, the system uses a best-first search strategy guided by GPT-4o, which evaluates candidates based on factors like performance metrics, training dynamics and the quality of the plots generated. The selected nodes are expanded by creating a new child node.
quotes.rationale: Identifies GPT-4o as the evaluator guiding best-first search and node expansion using metrics, traces, and plots.

quotes.quote: The agent processes the PDF of a manuscript to produce a structured review, including numerical scores for soundness, presentation and contribution, along with a list of strengths and weaknesses and a preliminary accept or reject decision (Supplementary Information section A.3).
quotes.rationale: Defines the manuscript-review evaluator and its terminal scoring and accept/reject outputs.

quotes.quote: We benchmarked The Automated Reviewer against human decisions using ICLR data from the publicly available OpenReview dataset33. The Automated Reviewer achieved a comparable balanced accuracy with humans (69% versus 66%; see Supplementary Information section A.3.2 for details) and a higher F1 score compared with inter-human group agreement (0.62 versus 0.49) in the NeurIPS 2021 consistency experiment34, for which roughly 10% of submissions were randomly selected and sent to two independent review committees, thus providing a real-world benchmark of inter-reviewer consistency (Table 1).
quotes.rationale: Reports discrimination and agreement evidence for the Automated Reviewer against human review outcomes and inter-human consistency.

quotes.quote: The overall process was then run to generate ideas, experiments and papers. We manually filtered the most promising outputs at each stage (Supplementary Information section A.4). Had this filtering not occurred, the papers under analysis would still have been produced in their final form, just along with other papers and, thus, at a greater total cost. This process resulted in three complete manuscripts being selected for submission.
quotes.rationale: Reports human selection of promising outputs and manuscripts for submission, while indicating it did not modify the generated final papers.

quotes.quote: The entire scientific workflow for each paper, from ideation and coding to manuscript writing, was performed without any human modification.
quotes.rationale: Constrains the human role to filtering/selection rather than direct modification of the scientific workflow artifacts.

quotes.quote: These three submissions were included among the 43 papers reviewed for the workshop. Reviewers were informed that some of the submissions were AI-generated but not which ones, ensuring a blind process.
quotes.rationale: Describes an external blind human review process for the submitted manuscripts.

diagnostics.consequence: Claims about closed-loop novelty filtering cannot be supported by the packet beyond literature-informed proposal refinement.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: To ensure the proposals are both grounded and new, this process is tightly integrated with a literature review module that queries external academic databases to identify knowledge gaps and avoid rediscovering existing work. The system uses structured prompts to guide idea generation and reflection rounds to refine proposals based on the literature search results (see Supplementary Information section A.2.6 for prompts).

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports no reliability study for the literature-review novelty channel.

diagnostics.consequence: Search may optimize toward internally judged promise, plot quality, and metrics without packet evidence that those judgments are independently validated.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: At the end of each stage, an LLM-based evaluator assesses all leaf nodes and selects the most promising one to serve as the root for the next stage of exploration, thus effectively pruning less promising research avenues.

During experimentation, the plots generated are fed to a VLM, which is prompted to act as a scientist and critique them. For example, it might flag nonsensical axes or issues in the quality of generated examples or suggest clearer ways to present the data. This feedback is used to generate new experimental nodes in the tree search aimed at addressing the identified issues.

When choosing between non-buggy nodes, the system uses a best-first search strategy guided by GPT-4o, which evaluates candidates based on factors like performance metrics, training dynamics and the quality of the plots generated. The selected nodes are expanded by creating a new child node.

diagnostics.statement: The implemented search-control evaluators are internal LLM/VLM channels with no packet-reported reliability evidence for their specific routing decisions.

diagnostics.consequence: The terminal reviewer benchmark should not be generalized to the ideation, plot critique, or search-control evaluators.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The agent processes the PDF of a manuscript to produce a structured review, including numerical scores for soundness, presentation and contribution, along with a list of strengths and weaknesses and a preliminary accept or reject decision (Supplementary Information section A.3).

We benchmarked The Automated Reviewer against human decisions using ICLR data from the publicly available OpenReview dataset33. The Automated Reviewer achieved a comparable balanced accuracy with humans (69% versus 66%; see Supplementary Information section A.3.2 for details) and a higher F1 score compared with inter-human group agreement (0.62 versus 0.49) in the NeurIPS 2021 consistency experiment34, for which roughly 10% of submissions were randomly selected and sent to two independent review committees, thus providing a real-world benchmark of inter-reviewer consistency (Table 1).

diagnostics.statement: Reliability evidence is reported for the Automated Reviewer terminal manuscript assessment, not for all validator channels.

diagnostics.consequence: End-to-end autonomy claims require separation between artifact generation and human selection because human filtering affected which outputs were submitted.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The overall process was then run to generate ideas, experiments and papers. We manually filtered the most promising outputs at each stage (Supplementary Information section A.4). Had this filtering not occurred, the papers under analysis would still have been produced in their final form, just along with other papers and, thus, at a greater total cost. This process resulted in three complete manuscripts being selected for submission.
The entire scientific workflow for each paper, from ideation and coding to manuscript writing, was performed without any human modification.

diagnostics.statement: The packet distinguishes no human modification of generated papers from human filtering and submission selection.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: At the end of each stage, an LLM-based evaluator assesses all leaf nodes and selects the most promising one to serve as the root for the next stage of exploration, thus effectively pruning less promising research avenues.

During experimentation, the plots generated are fed to a VLM, which is prompted to act as a scientist and critique them. For example, it might flag nonsensical axes or issues in the quality of generated examples or suggest clearer ways to present the data. This feedback is used to generate new experimental nodes in the tree search aimed at addressing the identified issues.

When choosing between non-buggy nodes, the system uses a best-first search strategy guided by GPT-4o, which evaluates candidates based on factors like performance metrics, training dynamics and the quality of the plots generated. The selected nodes are expanded by creating a new child node.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report channel-specific validation for the LLM/VLM evaluators that control stage transitions, plot-driven revisions, and best-first node expansion.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Each implemented search-control channel has its own reported validation result rather than inheriting evidence from another channel.
success_check.evidence_needed: Held-out traces or benchmark cases comparing evaluator decisions with predefined or independent judgments for each channel.
success_check.observable: Per-channel reliability table covering stage-end leaf selection, VLM plot critique, and GPT-4o best-first selection.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The agent processes the PDF of a manuscript to produce a structured review, including numerical scores for soundness, presentation and contribution, along with a list of strengths and weaknesses and a preliminary accept or reject decision (Supplementary Information section A.3).

We benchmarked The Automated Reviewer against human decisions using ICLR data from the publicly available OpenReview dataset33. The Automated Reviewer achieved a comparable balanced accuracy with humans (69% versus 66%; see Supplementary Information section A.3.2 for details) and a higher F1 score compared with inter-human group agreement (0.62 versus 0.49) in the NeurIPS 2021 consistency experiment34, for which roughly 10% of submissions were randomly selected and sent to two independent review committees, thus providing a real-world benchmark of inter-reviewer consistency (Table 1).

recommendations.proposed_action: Keep the Automated Reviewer benchmark scoped to terminal manuscript assessment unless additional evidence is reported for other validators.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: No search-control, novelty, or plot-critique channel cites the Automated Reviewer benchmark as its own reliability evidence.
success_check.evidence_needed: A channel-by-channel evidence map tying each reliability claim to the exact evaluator and target property tested.
success_check.observable: Reliability evidence matrix with Automated Reviewer evidence assigned only to terminal manuscript assessment.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The overall process was then run to generate ideas, experiments and papers. We manually filtered the most promising outputs at each stage (Supplementary Information section A.4). Had this filtering not occurred, the papers under analysis would still have been produced in their final form, just along with other papers and, thus, at a greater total cost. This process resulted in three complete manuscripts being selected for submission.
The entire scientific workflow for each paper, from ideation and coding to manuscript writing, was performed without any human modification.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report human filtering as selection authority separately from artifact modification in end-to-end workflow descriptions.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Human selection roles are disclosed without contradicting the no-modification claim.
success_check.evidence_needed: A workflow accounting that separates generated artifact creation from manual filtering and submission selection.
success_check.observable: Human-authority table distinguishing generator, executor, validator, memory, and policy roles.
